summary: Inflation cooled while growth stayed resilient and earnings revisions stabilized.
Policy was easy and growth re-accelerated after recession repair.
Growth slowed while inflation was still uncomfortable and credit was beginning to fray.
Funding pressure and rapidly widening spreads dominated the macro backdrop.
Policy support and stabilization in earnings revisions powered a recovery setup.
Growth wobbled even without a hot inflation problem because risk appetite was fragile.
Disinflation and improving revisions created a benign backdrop despite low nominal growth.
Tightening policy collided with softer global growth and weaker revisions.
The economy moved into an abrupt contraction with severe labor market damage.
Inflation stayed hot even as growth and revisions softened under tighter policy.
Inflation cooled materially while growth slowed but did not break.
Restrictive policy remained in place but revisions and activity stabilized.
Growth softened under still-restrictive policy and revisions turned weaker.
